commands.when_to_run: Before and after changes.
commands.success_signal: Exits 0 and lists changed files if any.
commands.recovery_hint: If this fails, confirm you are inside the modelarena Git checkout.

commands.when_to_run: Only when node_modules is missing or dependencies changed.
commands.success_signal: npm exits 0 and package-lock.json remains consistent.
commands.recovery_hint: Use npm because package-lock.json is the lockfile. Do not switch package managers.

commands.intent: run local Vite app and local API plugin
commands.when_to_run: When a browser preview or manual local smoke test is needed.
commands.recovery_hint: If port 3000 is busy, stop the existing server or run Vite with an explicit alternate port. The Vite API plugin replaces vercel dev for local work.

commands.when_to_run: Only when explicitly investigating Vercel CLI behavior.
commands.recovery_hint: src/server/README.md documents that vercel dev previously failed non-interactively; prefer npm run dev for ordinary local work.

commands.when_to_run: For most code changes.
commands.recovery_hint: Fix TypeScript errors in the changed area first; avoid broad refactors.

commands.when_to_run: Interactive local development only.
commands.success_signal: Vitest starts and reports test status.
commands.recovery_hint: For non-interactive agent verification, prefer test-run.

commands.when_to_run: For server, shared logic, and broad UI changes.
commands.recovery_hint: If failures predate your change, capture the failing tests and do not hide them.

commands.when_to_run: For frontend, routing, Vite config, public asset, and deploy-adjacent changes.
commands.success_signal: Vite build exits 0 and writes dist/.
commands.recovery_hint: dist/ is generated and ignored. Fix build errors at their source rather than editing dist/.

commands.when_to_run: After npm run build when verifying built routing/assets.
commands.recovery_hint: Run build first if preview cannot find dist/.

commands.when_to_run: When a stale dist/ build is confusing local verification.
commands.recovery_hint: Only removes generated dist/. Do not use broader rm commands.

commands.intent: generate Drizzle migration from schema diff
commands.when_to_run: After intentional schema changes in src/server/db/schema.ts.
commands.success_signal: A new migration appears in drizzle/ and meta snapshots update.
commands.recovery_hint: Review generated SQL carefully. Commit migration output with the schema change.

commands.when_to_run: Only after confirming DATABASE_URL targets the intended development database.
commands.recovery_hint: Do not run against an unknown or production DATABASE_URL without explicit approval.

commands.when_to_run: Rarely, for development-only schema experiments when explicitly approved.
commands.recovery_hint: This skips migration history. Prefer db-generate plus db-migrate.

commands.when_to_run: When manually inspecting a known development database.
commands.success_signal: Drizzle Studio starts, usually on port 4983.
commands.recovery_hint: Confirm DATABASE_URL and avoid exposing secrets in output.

commands.when_to_run: Only for a confirmed development database when demo data reset is intended.
commands.success_signal: Seed script exits 0 and prints created share slugs.
commands.recovery_hint: This truncates app tables. The script refuses NODE_ENV=production unless ALLOW_PROD_SEED=1, but still confirm the database target first.

commands.when_to_run: When a development database needs the curated starter persona library.
commands.recovery_hint: Idempotent by name, but still confirm DATABASE_URL. Production requires explicit override.

commands.when_to_run: Before deploys or when validating the target database migration state.
commands.recovery_hint: Requires DATABASE_URL and reads the target database. Confirm the environment before running.

commands.when_to_run: When desktop launcher icon assets need regeneration.
commands.success_signal: Icon assets are generated under ignored assets/icons/.
commands.recovery_hint: assets/icons/ is generated and ignored.

commands.when_to_run: When changing or testing desktop launcher scripts.
commands.success_signal: Desktop app bundle is generated under ignored desktop/.
commands.recovery_hint: desktop/ is generated and ignored. Rebuild if the repo path changes because the launcher bakes paths.

commands.when_to_run: Only when Christian wants the local desktop app installed.
commands.success_signal: The app is installed in the expected macOS location.
commands.recovery_hint: This changes local machine state. Ask before running.

commands.when_to_run: When the desktop launcher has a lingering local server or window.
commands.recovery_hint: Use when appified dev server processes persist after closing the window.
